title: Chrysopsis amygdalina
taxonomy: Chrysopsis amygdalina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysopsis, which is a group of plants that are native to the southeastern United States. The family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Chrysopsis amygdalina is a perennial herb that is often found in dry, sandy areas. Its classification is further supported by its phylogenetic relationships with other plants in the same family and genus.
uses: Chrysopsis amygdalina, also known as Needle Goldenrod, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. Its roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
